title: allomorphia howellii
intro: Allomorphia howellii is a plant species known as a flowering plant native to tropical Africa, particularly in countries such as Cameroon and the Democratic Republic of Congo. It is also known as the "African walnut" and its bark, leaves, and roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Allomorphia howellii include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and supporting respiratory health. In herbal preparations, Allomorphia howellii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional remedies and folk medicine.
